pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates this specific notebook path
task.json and export_result.sh instructions and checks
task.json and export_result.sh instructions and checks
setup_urbansim.sh copies the dataset to this path
task.json specifies 1.17% and export_result.sh verifies it

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the real San Francisco UrbanSim dataset (`sanfran_public.h5`), downloaded from the official UDST GitHub repository. The dataset contains actual building and parcel records. I verified via code execution that the ground truth calculation from `export_result.sh` expects a total assessed value of ~75.1 million and a total tax of ~879,350 across 188 zones, correctly applying the 1.17% (0.0117) tax rate specified in the prompt. The `residential_sales_price` column is used directly as the assessed value, despite appearing to be a per-square-foot metric in reality, as explicitly instructed by the task description. The top 5 zones by estimated annual tax are 184, 131, 168, 179, and 185. The required output paths (notebook, CSV, and PNG) and data paths exactly match the task description and verifier logic.
